Each context needs command endpoints (POST) that accept JSON, dispatch to command handlers, and return results, plus query endpoints (GET) that read projections. Routes nest at /api/v1/{context} (e.g., /api/v1/narrative, /api/v1/characters). Requires AppState to have EventRepository wired in.

description: Create the root +layout.svelte with: (1) Responsive app shell — sidebar or top nav, (2) Navigation links to major sections: Campaigns, Characters, Sessions, Play, (3) Tailwind-styled with consistent design tokens, (4) Proper HTML semantics (header, nav, main, footer), (5) SvelteKit preloading on hover (already configured). Keep it clean and minimal — this is a tabletop RPG tool, not a flashy game UI.

description: Implement command handlers for the 7 domain crates (narrative, rules, world-state, character, inventory, session, content). Each crate has an empty placeholder at src/application/command_handlers.rs. Handlers should accept Commands, validate them, produce DomainEvents, and persist via EventRepository. Follow the event-driven flow: Command -> CommandHandler -> DomainEvent(s) -> EventRepository.append_events(). Use TDD — write failing tests first.
design:
## Narrative Exemplar First

Implement otherworlds-narrative command handlers as the exemplar pattern, then replicate to remaining 6 crates in follow-up.

### Architecture Decisions
1. Function-based handlers: `async fn handle_*(cmd, &dyn Clock, &dyn EventRepository) -> Result<Vec<Event>, DomainError>`
2. Aggregate domain methods push events to `uncommitted_events`
3. `apply()` stays minimal (version increment only)
4. `apply_stored_event_version_only()` shortcut for reconstitution (full StoredEvent deserialization out of scope)
5. Sequence number: `version + uncommitted_events.len() + 1`
6. `causation_id` = `correlation_id` at command-to-event boundary

### Files to Modify
- `backend/crates/otherworlds-narrative/src/domain/aggregates.rs` — Add `advance_beat()`, `present_choice()` + tests
- `backend/crates/otherworlds-narrative/src/application/command_handlers.rs` — Add handler fns + mock tests
- `backend/crates/otherworlds-narrative/Cargo.toml` — Add tokio dev-dep

### TDD Steps
1. RED/GREEN: `advance_beat()` domain method on `NarrativeSession` → `BeatAdvanced` event
2. RED/GREEN: `present_choice()` domain method → `ChoicePresented` event
3. RED/GREEN: `handle_advance_beat()` async handler fn with MockEventRepository
4. RED/GREEN: `handle_present_choice()` async handler fn
5. Refactor: clippy, fmt, full test suite

### Tests: 4 total (2 aggregate unit tests + 2 handler unit tests)
title: Implement domain command handlers

description: Create a typed API client that runs server-side only (in +page.server.ts and +server.ts). Responsibilities: (1) Base fetch wrapper with API_BASE_URL from env, (2) Typed methods for each backend endpoint grouped by context, (3) Consistent error handling — parse ErrorResponse from backend, throw typed errors, (4) Request/response logging in dev mode, (5) Correlation ID forwarding. Use $env/static/private for API_BASE_URL. Place in $lib/server/api/ so SvelteKit prevents client-side import.
title: Implement server-side API client in $lib/server/

description: Add Tailwind CSS v4 to the SvelteKit project. Install @tailwindcss/vite plugin, configure vite.config.ts, add base CSS import to app.html or +layout.svelte. Verify with a simple utility class on the index page. Follow Tailwind v4's new CSS-first configuration approach (no tailwind.config.js — use @theme in CSS).
title: Install and configure Tailwind CSS v4 for SvelteKit
Rename Postgres container in Docker Compose config

description: Add an Arc<dyn EventRepository + Send + Sync> field to AppState in otherworlds-api so route handlers can access the event store. Instantiate PgEventRepository from the PgPool during server startup in main.rs. Currently AppState holds PgPool, Clock, and Rng but the repository is not wired in.

description: The event store supports optimistic concurrency via expected_version in append_events(), but no domain crate tests concurrent modification scenarios. Add tests that verify: (1) Two simultaneous commands on the same aggregate produce a ConcurrencyConflict error, (2) The conflict includes expected vs actual version info, (3) The API layer maps ConcurrencyConflict to HTTP 409. Can be tested at the domain handler level with RecordingEventRepository configured to return conflict errors.
title: Add optimistic concurrency conflict tests

description: The narrative route handlers (advance_beat, present_choice) generate a correlation_id but never log it. The technical manifesto (section 11) requires every request to have a traceable correlation ID. Add #[tracing::instrument] or tracing::info! calls to emit correlation_id and session_id at the HTTP boundary. This is the exemplar for handler tracing — other context handlers will follow this pattern.
title: Add tracing instrumentation to narrative API handlers

description: MockEventRepository, FixedClock, and MockRng are duplicated across otherworlds-narrative command_handlers tests, otherworlds-api state tests, and otherworlds-api narrative route tests. As more domain crates get implemented, this duplication will grow. Extract shared test mocks into a reusable location — either a test_support module in otherworlds-core or a dedicated otherworlds-test-support crate. This prevents mock divergence when trait signatures change.
title: Extract shared test mock infrastructure

close_reason: All 7 bounded contexts updated: Uuid::new_v4() replaced with rng.next_uuid() in domain methods, command handlers, and API routes. 352 tests pass, clippy clean.
description: All 7 domain crates use Uuid::new_v4() for event_id in domain methods, breaking the replay determinism manifesto constraint. Fix requires: (1) Extend DeterministicRng trait with a next_uuid() method, (2) Thread &mut dyn DeterministicRng through all domain methods alongside Clock, (3) Update all aggregate domain methods to use rng.next_uuid() instead of Uuid::new_v4(), (4) Update all command handler signatures to pass rng, (5) Update all tests to use SequenceRng or equivalent. Affects: narrative, character, rules, world-state, inventory, session, content aggregates.
title: Replace Uuid::new_v4() with DeterministicRng for event IDs
